entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content: @AdilHoussa if you are submitting B2B invoices through PA, please note that is incorrect process (this is for B2G). E-doc should be created belong Domestic customer invoices. Please refer to SAP Help (SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). I would recommend to watch webinar reply and use country-specific workzone for further exchange: https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/CK8AhwChqYjMisy8oYSfgv/workpage_tabs/LxnjcOht6DIhuitgncZLhH.

I don't know of any other pages except than my blog with the API demo code, the SAP API Hub site that you have already accessed and that link which is broken and we are working on fixing.

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
@MaeW  Yes. Electronic Invoicing via Peppol is former solution still required for B2G. Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform is the latest solution, for the mandate becoming effective in September. Also in this case, the invoice is sent to recipient through Peppol network.

You should follow Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform.

entry.title: The total balance of offsetting account after data migration is not zero.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

According to the posting logic during migration, the total balance of each migration clearing account or offsetting account, which is used for the offsetting postings, must be zero..

However, the data migration has already been completed, and I am now observing that the closing balances of the offsetting accounts are not zero across all company codes.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or have any idea what might be causing this?

Best regards,
Emal Rahman

entry.title: Re: How to show a comparison of price lists or last price...
entry.content:
Yes, this functionality is already available in standard SAP Business One.

When you are creating a Purchase Order, place the cursor in the Unit Price field and press Ctrl + Tab. This opens the Last Prices Report, where you can review the item's purchase price history.

The report allows you to:

  • View prices from different vendors.
  • See prices from previous purchasing documents (Purchase Orders, Goods Receipt POs, A/P Invoices, etc., depending on your settings and available history).
  • Compare historical purchase prices before selecting the appropriate one.

As far as I know, there is no standard option to add a "Price List" entry to the right-click context menu of the Unit Price field. If you specifically need that behavior, it will require a customization (for example, using the SAP Business One UI API or an add-on).

For most purchasing scenarios, however, Ctrl + Tab in the Unit Price field is the standard SAP Business One way to access the Last Prices Report.

entry.content:
Hi @apoonia02 , thanks for sharing this, really helpful.
Since we have FES deployment of Fiori, would enabling Fiori as a SaaS application in BTP also result in the same outcome for S/4HANA Public Cloud?
I'm sure exposing Fiori CDM data is not equivalent to enabling Fiori as a SaaS application.

BR,
Denzil

entry.title: Re: PR Workflow Issue
entry.content:
Hi @watertec ,

SAP has not added document type in the workflow, nor has it been placed on the roadmap.  The good news is that you can add it with a little bit of code and the extension points below.

Extension Points to Insert Code:

  1. SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF - using this extension point, you could create a new condition, simple, name such as "Requisition Document Type"
  2. SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL - using this extension point, you would evaluate it and import CDS view(s) released for Key User Extensibility - such as I_PURCHASEREQUISITIONAPI01 / Document type

Then, use the standard flexible workflow definition apps - namely Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions. The new condition will appear as part of #1.  Then it will assess based upon #2

Please reward points and deem as "Answered" if this helped! 😃

entry.title: Missing descriptions when downloading the service catalog
entry.content:
Hello,

We have noticed that when downloading the service catalog in SAP for ME, the descriptions are missing. The entries are included in the download, but the corresponding description texts are not.

Any idea?

Thank you and best regards,
Frank

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Project Error
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I'm currently learning ABAP, as I'm following the learning journey of acquiring core abap skills, I created a cloud project on eclipse, and got access to a practice system from SAP, I created a cloud project and entered the URL of the system, However, I deleted this project entirely and created a new one, but the name of the old project is still there as system id,

 

Now when I try to edit something in the new project, I get error message object could not be locked, objects are assigned to provided repository "zlearning", changes are not allowed.

 

What can I do now to solve this problem?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: From Classical SAP to AI Architect: Solving Business Bottlenecks with Gen AI and the BTP Framework
entry.content:
Join us for an exciting SAP Community Nigeria session: "From Classical SAP to AI Architect: Solving Business Bottlenecks with Generative AI and the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework on SAP BTP for S/4HANA".

Whether you're a classical SAP professional just starting your AI journey or looking to deepen your BTP knowledge, this session is for you. We'll break down what AI and BTP mean for classical modules and how you can begin pivoting your skills into this space - followed by a real-world success story of how the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework was used to automate complex sales orders and solve major operational bottlenecks.

Date: 22nd July 2026 | Time: 3PM WAT | Location: Virtual

Reserve your spot  here 

SAP Community 

@SAP

entry.title: Leading the Way for Post-Quantum Cryptography
entry.content:
By Sandip Dholakia, Chair Cryptography Innovation Hub, and Volkmar Lotz, Project Lead Crypto Agility Initiative, SAP


Quantum technologies are advancing rapidly, leading to a cryptographically relevant quantum computer that can break today’s public-key cryptography, which will be available sooner rather than later.

Like major industry peers, SAP is aware of the increased risk and is striving to be post-quantum ready by 2029 across its products, services and infrastructure. SAP has established a Cryptography Center or Excellence (CoE) that governs the migration process, maintains a cryptographic inventory, and coordinates migration projects across the company following a roadmap recognizing technical and business priorities for the identified cryptographic assets. Protecting customer data and privacy remains SAP’s highest priority, and to support this commitment, the SAP cryptography CoE has adopted a long-term strategy focused on cryptographic agility rather than one-off migration projects.

 

The Risk

Quantum computers can solve certain mathematical problems faster than classical computers. In 1994, Peter Shor demonstrated that a sufficiently powerful quantum computer could efficiently factor large integers and solve discrete logarithm problems. This breakthrough directly threatens widely used public-key cryptography algorithms, including RSA, Diffie-Hellman, and Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) as their security relies on the difficulty of solving these problems with a classical computer. These algorithms form the foundation of many security mechanisms used across the industry, including within SAP products.


Why the Timeline Matters

Although the cybersecurity professionals understood the threat for decades, momentum accelerated after the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) proposed a timeline in 2024 to introduce Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) alongside traditional cryptography, starting in 2030 and fully phasing out traditional cryptography by 2035. This timeline is challenged by recent advances in quantum technologies: accelerated hardware development, improved quantum error correction and optimizations for Shor’s algorithm suggest that the gap between existing quantum computers and those needed to break public-key cryptography is closing. Considering these developments, SAP has raised the ambition to replace the current algorithms by quantum-resilient ones across its products, services and infrastructures by 2029.

This reflects an industry trend: Earlier in 2026, major industry peers, Google and Microsoft among them, announced plans to complete their PQC migration by 2029. In June 2026, the White House issued Executive Order 14412, requiring High Value Assets (HVAs) within U.S. federal agencies to be quantum-safe by the end of 2030.


The Challenge

History shows that replacing cryptographic standards can take years, sometimes decades. Cryptographic protocols are deeply embedded in applications, infrastructure, communications, and operational processes. Even small changes can have far-reaching and unintended consequences. As a result, organizations often hesitate to take immediate action to perform necessary upgrades, increasing exposure to evolving threats.

SAP leverages cryptographic agility to implement a sustainable approach to meet evolving cryptographic requirements.


Cryptographic Agility at SAP

SAP’s strategy is built around cryptographic agility—the ability to adapt quickly to future cryptographic changes without major disruption. SAP is implementing a phased enterprise-wide approach:

• Governance: The Cryptography CoE provides executive oversight, establishes milestones, defines success metrics, and guides product teams.
• Inventory: Teams identify cryptographic assets and generate Cryptographic Bills of Materials (CBoMs) using industry-standard tools and CycloneDX formats.
• Prioritization: Assets are ranked based on criticality to guide migration planning. This takes into account that some types of attacks can be launched already today, for instance, collecting encrypted data in transit for later decryption with a quantum computer (“harvest now, decrypt later”)
• Implementation: PQC algorithms are introduced in phases, following established roadmaps reflecting the prioritization.
• Validation: New implementations undergo rigorous testing before production deployment.

Over time, these capabilities will become automated and embedded into SAP’s Secure Software Development and Operations Lifecycle (SecureSDOL).


SAP’s Position

• Taking recent developments in quantum technology into account, SAP strives to become post-quantum cryptography ready by 2029.
• SAP is actively advancing toward quantum resistance and aligning with emerging regulatory and industry expectations. The company’s primary focus is on adopting NIST-recommended PQC algorithms while remaining open to other industry-proven solutions where appropriate.
• SAP has already released an updated Common Cryptographic Library (v8.6.4) that includes PQC capabilities for product teams. At present, SAP does not anticipate significant performance or cost impacts for customers, partners, or vendors during the migration.

SAP’s objective is: protect products, services, and customer data against evolving threats while delivering secure, resilient, and future-ready solutions in the post-quantum era.

Can't you just use the normal create to create the instance of business object B and read the data from Business Object A during the unmanaged save? Can you clarify the requirements with an actual business object and tell us how/where are you calling the static action from?

Hi! 

Make sure to click on the edit button on the bottom left side. It's a little hidden. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: S4/H Public Cloud - Run statutory reports
entry.content:
Hi Sam,

Short answer: No — Quantity and Unit of Measure cannot be added to the GSTR-3B output, because GSTR-3B is a summary-level return that has no line-item Quantity/UOM by legal design. Customizing it via Define Statutory Reports will not change this.

Is adding Quantity and UOM to the GSTR-3B output supported in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

No. GSTR-3B is a consolidated summary return. On the GST portal, it reports only aggregated taxable value and tax amounts per section — there is no field for quantity or UOM anywhere in the form (GST portal – Form GSTR-3B FAQ). In SAP, the GSTR3B report tied to report category IN_GSTR3B_DCL is generated from Tax Box Mappings and Tax Groups, i.e. aggregated tax values — not from a document line-item list, so Quantity/UOM are not part of its data model (Configuration for GSTR3B Report).

Can I achieve this by customizing GSTR-3B with Define Statutory Reports?

No, not for the legal GSTR-3B. The Define Statutory Reports app does exist in Public Cloud (business catalog SAP_FIN_BC_SRF_CONF_PC) and lets you copy or extend report definitions, create report categories/activities, and even use a Custom Reusable Library as a query source (Define Statutory Reports, Custom Reusable Library – Statutory Reporting).

Note: The report definition is bound to the legal file format requested by the authority, and you can only surface attributes that the underlying released data sources actually expose. Since the GSTR-3B format has no Quantity/UOM node and the tax-box source data is aggregated, there is nothing to map those columns to. The blogs you saw describe the generic extensibility framework, not a supported way to add Quantity/UOM to GSTR-3B specifically.

Where do Quantity and UOM actually belong for GST?

In GSTR-1, not GSTR-3B. Quantity and UOM (as UQC, Unit Quantity Code) are required in Table 12 – HSN-wise summary of outward supplies of GSTR-1 (GST portal – Table 12 HSN advisory). SAP's standard GSTR1 Report in Public Cloud covers this HSN/UQC summary, so if this is the compliance need, the GSTR-1 report is the correct place to look (GSTR1 Report).

Verify: If you genuinely need a quantity/UOM view for internal reconciliation (not the statutory filing), build a separate custom analytical report on released line-item CDS views — keep it outside the GSTR-3B statutory object so you don't break the legal file.

Summary

Quantity/UOM in GSTR-3B is not supported and not applicable — the return is a tax-box summary with no such fields, and Define Statutory Reports cannot inject them. If you need HSN quantity/UQC for compliance, use the standard GSTR-1 report (Table 12). For any internal quantity analysis, create a separate custom report rather than modifying GSTR-3B.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,
Michal

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Beginners: Everything You Need to Know - All at one stop!
entry.content: Its really very informative. thanks a ton.

entry.title: Re: Context Aware Data Action Scoping
entry.content:
Hi @thomaselit,

The data action in edit mode is shown above.   The story trigger for the data action will not expose the parameter in this case, because the data filter is private.

Thanks,
Scott

entry.title: GitHub Integration Access and Configuration for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I would like to request information regarding the GitHub integration feature available in SAP Integration Suite.

In the Integration Suite interface, I can see Git Push and Git Pull options. However, I do not have the necessary configuration options or documentation to use this feature.

I am looking for guidance on the following:

  1. How do I connect SAP Integration Suite to a GitHub repository?

  2. How do I authenticate or log in to GitHub from my SAP Integration Suite tenant?

  3. Where can I find or configure the Git repository URL for my Integration Suite environment?

  4. Are there any roles, authorizations, or tenant configurations that must be enabled before Git integration becomes available?

  5. Are there any prerequisites on the SAP BTP or Integration Suite side to enable this functionality?

  6. Is this feature available with our current SAP Integration Suite Enterprise Standard Edition subscription, or does it require an additional

entry.title: Concern with program asset Year-End Closing program in S/4
entry.content:
We recently updated to S/4 HANA for our SAP systems. However we noticed that we are still using the old asset year end closing program RAJABS00 instead of FAA_CLOSE_FISCAL_YEARS.

Our question is, is there any problem if we continue to use RAJABS00 instead of FAA_CLOSE_FISCAL_YEARS for asset year end closing in S/4 HANA?

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content: @BehzadPouyanfar Certifications cannot be transfered, they are stored in the SAP Universal ID. If you required additional support with this, please contact the  CIC via Phone, Email or Chat and we will be happy to assist you.

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content: @rettyvel Please engage with CIC via Phone, Email or Chat and we will be happy to assist you, share your Ticket ID number from the created ticket in the Learning Helpcenter.

entry.title: Re: GROW with SAP, Resource 1 Pager (Base & Premium)
entry.content:
Hi Andrew, thanks for putting this together—it's a great reference. 

Thank you!

entry.title: BTP Solution Architect Certification guidance
entry.content:
Dear BTP Experts-

I have been looking into the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification path. While researching, I found that there are many blogs, YouTube channels, and learning resources available, but they are scattered across different places. I was wondering if there is a structured learning path or course that follows the right sequence and is effective for certification preparation.

If you have already completed the SAP Certification– SAP BTP  Solution Architect certification, or if you are currently preparing for it, could you please recommend the best approach or learning path? I would really appreciate your guidance on what worked well for you.   SAP Learning Platform has courses in free. Were that sufficient or I need to focus on any other.

If there are any additional resources, structured courses,  mock exams, or YouTube channels that you found particularly helpful, I'd be grateful if you could share them.

Thank you in advance!

Best Regards,

Pramod Upadhyay

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) Migration in SAP SuccessFactors: Common Errors and Resolution Guid
entry.content: JBP migration needs to be completed first before TIH migration even if instance is new.

entry.title: Custom Account Assignment Category not worked with Item Category Enhanced
entry.content:
Currently, we are using custom account assignment categories for Cost center, project & Assets. It worked well with Blank Account assignment category but not worked with Item Category Enhanced (E) 

Combination item category E/account assignment category L not defined

Message no. ME066

entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades: Continuing the Journey from Preparation to Execution
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrade Success Series continues this summer with two new sessions focused on how customers can use SAP Joule for Consultants and SAP Cloud ALM to teams move faster and work more consistently across the upgrade lifecycle.

Whether you're preparing for an upcoming upgrade or looking to make upgrade more structured and easier to follow across teams, these sessions provide practical guidance and demonstrations that can be applied directly to your own upgrade projects.  

Register Now

Whether you're joining the series for the first time or continuing from Part 1, the registration page provides access to previous session recordings and registration for the upcoming webinars.

Building on the Foundation

In June, we kicked off the SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrade Success Series with sessions covering:

  • How to plan your SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrade
  • Improving upgrade readiness with SAP Readiness Check
  • Streamlining upgrade planning and execution with Release Upgrade Management  

Together, these sessions focused on helping customers establish a practical starting point for planning and preparing upgrade projects. If you missed Part 1 catch the replays here.

Part 2 of the Upgrade Success Series is designed to help customers build on the foundations established in Part 1 and explore additional tools and approaches that can help improve planning and strengthen execution across the upgrade lifecycle.

 

July 29: SAP Joule for Consultants: Your AI Companion for SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades

Upgrade projects involve many planning, analysis, and coordination activities, and teams need clear guidance to keep the work moving.

This session introduces SAP Joule for Consultants and demonstrates how it can help accelerate and strengthen activities across the upgrade lifecycle.  

What You'll Learn

  • Use SAP Joule for Consultants Across Upgrade Activities – See how it provides guidance and assistance across key upgrade activities, helping teams navigate complex upgrade projects.
  • Build a Stronger Business Case for Upgrades – Learn how the solution helps identify innovations across SAP releases and supports ROI-focused discussions with business stakeholders.
  • Establish Consistent Upgrade Practices – Explore how it can support structured, repeatable upgrade routines to improve planning, execution, and governance.
  • Support Technical Readiness Assessments – See how the solution can assist with Simplification Item analysis, Compatibility Scope checks, add-on evaluation, and custom code analysis.
  • Experience SAP Joule for Consultants in Action – Review real-world upgrade scenarios and live demonstrations showcasing how it supports customers and consultants throughout the upgrade journey.

August 5: SAP Cloud ALM in Action: Managing SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades

The second session explores how SAP Cloud ALM can support customers throughout their upgrade journey and serve as a central solution for managing SAP Cloud ERP Private upgrade activities.  

What You'll Learn

  • Manage the End-to-End Upgrade Journey – See how Cloud ALM supports every phase of your upgrade, covering Clean Core, Project Management, and Test Management.
  • Accelerate Upgrade Planning and Execution – Leverage SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades with SAP Cloud ALM for streamlined project and requirements management.
  • Achieve and Maintain Clean Core Compliance – Use the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard to assess custom code and plan effective remediation aligned with SAP’s Clean Core principles.
  • Manage Regression Testing Effectively – Explore SAP Cloud ALM Test Management capabilities to plan, execute, and monitor regression testing activities during upgrades.
  • Support Post-Upgrade Success – Leverage monitoring capabilities to validate system performance and operational readiness after go-live.
  • Explore What's Next – Gain insight into planned innovations on the SAP Cloud ALM roadmap designed to further support future upgrade projects.

Ready for Part 2?

The next phase of the Upgrade Success Series explores how SAP Joule for Consultants and SAP Cloud ALM can support Cloud ERP Private upgrade projects.

If you missed Part 1, revisit our previous blog for an overview of the sessions and key topics covered.

Access the recordings and register for upcoming sessions:

Register Now

entry.title: Re: RAP managed early internal numbering and static factory action - retrieve automatic guid
entry.content:
Hi,

thanks for your quick reply.

Yes, maybe I left out too much of my final vision.

Business Object B is a generic representation of different XML-messages coming in from and going out to an interface.
As there are several possible message types  with different XML content, I created a generic header and  a generic content table. Those combined are forming the Business Object of messages to that interface. 
I planned a different factory method for every possible XML message type. Each one creates the instance (1 line each per head and content table, each with the same message guid), map the data from the external Business Object A (or sometimes another Business Object C, depending on the factory method and therefor XML message type) and then store the message content and send the message.

When relying of the standard create methods any caller has to know about the internal structure of the Business Object B which collides with clean code principles. In this specific case the caller has to know that when creating an instance it has to be done for head and content synchronously. With own factory method(s) I can hide that from the caller.

Just when writing this reply I get the idea that possibly I can split the methods. The sending part I should definitely carve out of the factory method as it is generic for all messages and only needed for outgoing messages.
But the dependence of creation and mapping stays. A caller should not need to know that after creation another map method has to be called. And even worse: for an existing instance a map method should never be called again. So the mapping is a crucial part of creating a XML message. And as I need to embed the message guid in one field of the XML structure (senders messasge id) I need the message guids generated by the RAP framework.

Hope that clarifies it a bit.

Michael

entry.title: Re: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation
entry.content: Nice blog, Jan!

entry.title: Re: Unable to auto create POs from source assigned PRs in...
entry.content:
Hello!

In a source list, a source of supply can be one of two types for a material/plant:

  • A vendor (info record/contract/scheduling agreement), entered in the Vendor field
  • An internal plant (stock transfer), entered in the PPl (Procurement Plant) field, with no vendor number or outline agreement entered

The field that controls whether MRP will automatically pick a source and assign it to the generated PR is the "MRP" indicator (called "Materials Planning" in the Manage Source Lists Fiori app). Setting it to 1 means that source is selected during the MRP run and assigned to the purchase requisition automatically.

Only one active record for a given material/plant/validity-period can carry MRP indicator = 1. The fixed vendor indicator in the source list is not considered by MRP; only the MRP indicator is considered and must have only one source with this MRP indicator. If is added a second MRP-relevant record for the same period, the system shows the "Active Source List exists" message.

External vendor record already have MRP = 1 for material/plant NAU98113G/GP01. When the supplying plant is added (GP01, PPl field) as a second MRP-relevant source for the same period, the system rejects it, not because it's a "fixed" source conflict, but because of this MRP-indicator uniqueness rule.

The plant is only determined as the source of supply for a purchase requisition item with item type U (stock transfer), and for PRs created in the planning run, the material requires a corresponding special procurement type in the MRP data, this part is right (special procurement key + storage location config is what puts the supplying plant/storage location on the PR item). But the automatic PO creation job still relies on the source list to formally set the PR's "source determined" flag. Since the MRP-relevant record for that material/plant is the external vendor, the system never assigns the internal supplying-plant source at PR creation time, so the PR is created, but without a confirmed unique source and the auto-PO job skips it.

 

Recommended fix:

  • Change the external vendor's source list record: set its MRP/"Materials Planning" indicator to blank ("Not relevant"), it just stops competing for automatic MRP source determination.
  • Add a new record with the PPl field = GP01 (supplying plant) and MRP indicator = 1. This becomes the unique MRP-relevant source, so STO PRs will get the plant assigned automatically at creation and scheduled auto-PO job will pick them up.
  • Trade-off to flag internally: with the vendor record no longer MRP-relevant, MRP will no longer auto-propose that vendor onto regular (external) purchase requisitions for this material, those PRs would need a vendor assigned manually.

Alternatives:

  • Quota arrangement (Manage Quota Arrangements) with both sources set to MRP = 2: technically allows two MRP-relevant sources, but quota arrangements split requirements by percentage/quantity, they don't know a requirement came from an STO vs. a normal demand, so this won't reliably route STO-driven PRs to the plant and vendor-driven PRs to the vendor.
  • Splitting validity periods between the two records.
  • Turning off "Source List Requirement" at material/plant level: removes the whole control, meaning any buyer could pick any vendor without a source list check.

 

References:

https://blog.sap-press.com/source-lists-in-sap-s4hana

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/fixed-vendor-in-source-list-not-recognized-by-mrp/qaq-p/7589287

2340061 - Source list and MRP areas SAP ERP & S/4HANA

https://learning.sap.com/courses/purchasing-in-sap-s-4hana/controlling-source-determination-with-source-lists-1

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud 2608 / Transfer of Custom Field into...
entry.content:
Hello!

The Billing Due List Item pulls data in real time from the sales order (same underlying object reference), so field shows up "for free." The actual Billing Document is a new, separate document created via copy control, and copy control only moves standard SAP fields automatically. Custom fields need their own explicit transfer logic, even though enabled the same field on SD_BILLINGDOC, SD_BILLINGDOCITEM, and SD_BILLINGINDEX.

Recommended fix: implement a Custom Logic (BAdI) for data transfer to billing documents by accessing the configuration "Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing Documents", adding a new entry for the data transfer routine, and assigning a routine number between 3000000 and 3009999.

Then in the Custom Fields and Custom Logic app implement a BAdI where:

  • Mandatory: copy the importing parameters to the changing parameters using MOVE-CORRESPONDING for the billing document, billing document item, and item control structures.
  • Add actual field logic: select the custom field value from the referenced sales order item (using the reference sales document/item fields on the billing item) and write it into the corresponding custom field on the billing document item.

The logic would look like:

MOVE-CORRESPONDING bil_doc TO bil_doc_res.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING bil_doc_item TO bil_doc_item_res.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING bil_doc_item_contr TO bil_doc_item_contr_res.

* Get custom field value from the referenced sales order item

* Write it into the billing document item field

 

References:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/copying-control-from-sales-documents-to-billing-documents-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/14083320

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/0f52c40702114eb888f826d5e416d778.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Register now for the 2608 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
Hello, where will the recording of these sessions be available. 

Thank you,

Sneha

entry.title: Re: Confirmation Required: Released API/CDS View for Cust...
entry.content:
Hello!

I_CustUnivHierarchy and I_CustUnivHierarchyNode are read-only master data views. They do not expose any delta-relevant metadata, no creation timestamp, last-changed timestamp, deletion indicator, or change type. There is therefore no native way to determine, from these views alone, which records are new, changed, or deleted since the last extraction.

This is even though the Manage Customer Hierarchies Fiori app displays a change history to end users, meaning the underlying data changes are tracked somewhere in the system;

SAP's standard Change Document framework captures who changed what, when, and via a change-type flag (Insert / Update / Delete), and even supports delta reads by date/time. However, this path is not available for custom integration in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This is confirmed by:

  • SAP KBA 3535056 - "No released CDS View available for Change Documents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition”
  • SAP Help Portal documentation on Change Documents: the standard "Show Change Documents" app (which does support delta reads via date/time filters) warns that it gives access to all available change documents, with no way to restrict this to a user's specific organizational authorization area, meaning every user with access to the app can see change data outside what they're normally authorized for. This is precisely the risk SAP avoids by not releasing a general-purpose API for it.

We also identified a Fiori Reuse Library for Change Documents (backed by an OData service, APS_CHANGE_DOCUMENTS_SRV), but this is documented as a way to embed a change-history UI widget inside a custom Fiori app, not as an integration API  and would carry the same authorization-scoping problem if used outside that context.

 

Exist the alternative to use Change Data Capture (CDC) on a Custom CDS View:  can be used to add delta capability to a CDS view that doesn't natively have one. It works via real-time database triggers: INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE on the underlying tables is captured and made available as a delta feed.

SAP's confirms CDC for data extraction scenarios (like SAP BW, SAP Datasphere, or similar analytical/replication targets). It does not explicitly confirm or rule out whether a CDC-enabled custom CDS view can be exposed as a custom point-to-point OData API.

 

References:

SAP KBA 3535056  -  No released CDS View available for Change Documents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Help Portal - Change Documents (Show Change Documents app, CA_SHOW_CD)

SAP Help Portal - How to Use the Fiori Reuse Library for Change Document Solution

SAP Help Portal - View Browser app documentation (Data Extraction-Enabled Views, CDS view extensibility)

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/utilizing-standard-cds-views-for-change-document-tables-cdhdr-cdpos/ba-p/13573692

entry.title: Re: No WH Taks after GR from Production
entry.content:
Hello!

The extra Warehouse Task is not controlled by the Warehouse Process Type, it's controlled by the Putaway Control Indicator, from the Warehouse Product master and drives a separate determination step: the Storage Type Search Sequence. When GR is posted, the system determines a putaway destination using: Putaway Control Indicator (from the warehouse product) + Party Entitled to Dispose + Stock Type. This combination points to a Storage Type Search Sequence, which can list more than one storage type in order (e.g. first an interim/GR-zone storage type, then final storage type). If that sequence has two storage types in it, EWM creates two Warehouse Tasks, one hop into the interim storage type, then a second hop from there to the final bin. This is standard, by-design behavior.

Changing the Warehouse Process Type doesn't affect this because it governs a different determination (source area/staging, confirmation rules, movement categorization), not which storage types get searched for the destination bin.

The fix:

  • Identify the Putaway Control Indicator assigned to the finished product on the Warehouse Product master (Warehouse/Storage view of the product).
  • Go to the Storage Type Search configuration for putaway
  • Look up the sequence tied to that indicator (in combination with Party Entitled to Dispose / Stock Type for production GR scenario).
  • If the sequence lists two storage types, that's the extra task.

If this is the case, recommended options are:

  1. Remove the interim storage type from the sequence, so it contains only one target storage type - GR posts directly, one WT only.
  2. Assign a different Putaway Control Indicator to the product, one that's mapped to a single-storage-type sequence, if other products still need the two-step logic and you don't want to touch the shared sequence. 

If the sequence already shows only one storage type:

Then the "additional" task isn't a two-step putaway of the same stock, it's a separate task (different reference/trigger). In that case the cause is likely Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC), which defines an explicit multi-step storage process (e.g. deconsolidation - quality - putaway) rather than a simple storage type search. Check whether a Storage Process is assigned for this Warehouse Process Type/product combination, if a process with more than one step is active, each step generates its own task by design. Removing the unwanted step from the Storage Process definition (or reassigning the product/process type combination to a simpler one-step process) resolves it the same way.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Cloud: Q2 2026 Innovation Highlights
entry.content:
Hi @Saurabh-Kabra ,

The Offline SAP Retail Execution App is not part of the standard SAP Sales Cloud mobile app. Customers must first have SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, as it is a prerequisite for purchasing the Field Sales add-on. The Field Sales add-on includes the separate SAP Retail Execution (REX) mobile app, which provides the offline retail execution capabilities.

Thanks,
Sindy

entry.title: Re: Automatic Payment Run splits payment into two bank tr...
entry.content:
Hello!

Reference: SAP KBA 3555465 – "Incorrect Splitting of Payment Documents" (Public Edition)

Payment documents are split when any of the following differ between open items of the same supplier, even if payment method, company code, and bank account otherwise look identical briefly. The document type (KR vs. RE) itself is not a grouping criterion, most likely because the RE invoice went through a different posting path (Logistics Invoice Verification/MIRO) that derived one of these fields differently than the manually posted KR invoices.

Places to check:

  1. Partner bank type: This value must match across all items to be paid together, since it drives which house bank account is used. If the RE document is a supplier credit memo, note that the Partner Bank Type field is suppressed on credit memo entry, but if it was created in Create Supplier Invoice / Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced by referencing an original invoice (Invoice Reference + Year), the terms, including cash discount/payment terms and Partner Bank Type, are copied from the referenced invoice.
  2. Payment method and payment terms at item level: Compare directly on each open item (Manage Supplier Line Items), even if the overall payment method looks the same in the proposal. Payment terms differences can be splitting cause too, not just payment method.
  3. House Bank / House Bank Account:  Same comparison, at item level.
  4. Currency / Receiver (payee), part of the core grouping structure
  5. Items with vs. without a payment method entered in the document: items that contain a payment method in the document can’t be offset against items with no payment method entered in the document, purely for technical reasons (point 5). Worth checking whether the RE item has payment method populated at document level while the KR items rely on the payment program to select it (or vice versa).

entry.title: Re: Custom Account Assignment Category not worked with It...
entry.content:
Hello!

Item category E (Enhanced Limit) is hardcoded to only accept a fixed set of account assignment categories, independent of what's maintained in Customizing.

Supported account assignment categories for item category E:

C – Sales order

K – Cost center

P – Project

S – Third-party project

U – Unknown

N – Network

A – Asset

F – Order

Custom account assignment categories for Cost Center, Project, and Asset fail because they are not K, P, or A.

SAP Note 3645782 delivers a correction instruction removing this restriction entirely. Since this is a code correction, not a config switch, and Public Cloud does not allow self-service SNOTE implementation, can’t apply Note 3645782.

Standard approach  is to only use account assignment categories K, P, A  with item category E.

References:

KBA 3539424 - Error ME066 During Purchase Requisition Creation with Custom Account Assignment Category and Item Category E in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:  confirms SSCUI 101655 maintenance alone doesn't resolve it.

KBA 3293207 - Error ME066 for Enhanced Limit Item Category - SAP S/4HANA 1709 – 2023: Confirms the same restriction on-premise/Private Cloud; states the fix is standard as of 2025 FPS0, and points to Note 3645782 for earlier releases.

SAP Note 3645782 - Error ME/066 for Enhanced Limit Items in PO and PR: The actual correction instruction (SNOTE) that removes the account-assignment-category restriction for item category E.

KBA 2913747 - Item Category Enhanced Limit in Lean Services Procurement - SAP S/4HANA: Master reference listing all aprox.19 documented Enhanced Limit (item category E) restrictions by design; the ME066 issue is item #7 on this list.

KBA 2770340 - "Overall Limit"/"Expected Value" fields missing or not editable

KBA 2948320 - Error MEPO168 for Enhanced Limit with contract/RFQ reference

KBA 3405706 - Error ME261 in ME59N for Enhanced Limit

KBA 3396584 - Differences and relevant tables for External Services vs. Lean Services

KBA 3227060 - Procurement Lean Service Fiori Apps standard behaviors guide

entry.title: Re: Why Is My Content Marked as Spam?
entry.content: @jerryjanda  @AnnePetteroe  Not sure if this is still a protocol to inform here for highlighting the spammed blogs. But can you guys help please.

entry.title: Re: Future-Dated Records on Terminated Employees: 1H 2026 Finally Closes a Long-Standing Gap.
entry.content:
Hi,

May I confirm if this applies to One Time Payment (nonRecurringPay)? It only says Job Info and Comp Info, but future terminated employees also could have this data. Thanks.

entry.title: Extensibility Options for Fiori App F4419 – Manage Requests for Long-Term Supplier’s Declarations
entry.content:
We are currently reviewing the extensibility options for SAP Fiori app F4419 – Manage Requests for Long-Term Supplier’s Declarations.

Technical details:

  • BSP Application: LLS_LTSDREQ_MNG
  • OData Service: LLS_LTSDREQUEST_MNG 
  • Application Component: FT_LEG_PRE_SDM

The SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library does not show any documented extension points for this app.

We have two requirements:

  1. We need to save additional custom information in a custom table when a supplier declaration request is created. Is there a supported BAdI, enhancement spot, business event, API, or backend extension point available during or after request creation?
  2. We need to control or limit the number of agreements included in a request, possibly based on the logged-in user or on a value selected by the user through a pop-up before creation. Is there a supported way to implement this through a backend validation or a Fiori elements/UI extension?
  3. If 1 and 2 are possible, are these done via SEGW or via ABAP RAP?

We are looking for an upgrade-safe solution and would prefer to avoid modifying SAP standard UI5, OData, or backend objects.

entry.title: Re: The total balance of offsetting account after data mi...
entry.content:
Hello!

Each migration object posts its offsetting entry to its own dedicated offset account independently:

FI - Accounts receivable open item - Initial FI-AR offset account

FI - Accounts payable open item - Initial FI-AP offset account

FI - G/L account balance and open/line item - Initial other GL offset account

(If applicable) other open-item G/L balances - Initial other Open Item GL offset

There is no automatic cross-clearing mechanism between these independent migration objects. Additionally, the G/L balance migration object can’t post directly to AR/AP reconciliation accounts (error F5354, per KBA 3091350), this is by design, to preserve GL-to-subledger reconciliation. So the objects, and their offset accounts stay separate.

The offset accounts exist only as a temporary reconciliation/verification tool, their purpose is to confirm totals balance between source and target during migration, not to function as permanent zero-balance accounts.

Resolution: Manual Journal Entry Required

  • Verify the net total is zero across all offset accounts using Display G/L Account Balances. If the combined total isn't zero, investigate for missing migration data or an imbalanced legacy trial balance first.
  • Post a clearing journal entry via Post General Journal Entries (F0718) to offset the balances across all the offset accounts against each other, bringing each to zero.
  • Re-verify all offset accounts show zero afterward.

This is the standard, expected post-migration cleanup step, not a workaround.

 

References:

KBA 3757771 (primary)

KBA 3091350 - Direct postings to reconciliation accounts not allowed (error F5354)

KBA 2853964 - Migration Cockpit: Collective KBA for G/L Open Item/Balance or FI AP/AR Open Item

SAP Help Portal - FI - G/L account balance and open/line item, "Out of Scope" item 7

entry.title: Re: AADSTS90072 when accessing newly assigned SAP Practic...
entry.content:
Hi Shunelei

I am facing the same problem, since last week I tried to assign a new SAP practice system and can not due the Error AADSTS90072. I opened a case, but no response, a huge delay to response my cases, frustrated ... I book that week to study to my certification and I lost the time.

I recommend also, opening a case, there is a delay, but is the only way to have support, unfortunately

Regards

Wellington

entry.title: Re: Unfair Result in C_S4CFI_2602 – System-Based Exam Com...
entry.content: Its very frustrated, even you effort to improves, its hard without a explanation what exactly was our errors to be able to understand and prepare to next attempt, but without feedback no way to improve

entry.title: Re: BTP Solution Architect Certification guidance
entry.content:
Hi @pramodu  - SAP BTP Solution Architect certification, you can bring a mindset similar to how you prepare for a technical job interview; this will be exactly a replica of that.

In a job interview, the interviewer asks questions like how you configure Integration Suite services, how to enable HANA Cloud, Joule on BTP, etc. Most of the questions are practical-related, and there are very few theory questions.

One important thing: the interviewer in this exam is Artificial Intelligence. It will ask follow-up questions based on how you answer them.

Preparation: Follow the Learning Journey and get some hands-on experience with BTP. If you are a technical person, it is a piece of cake and super easy.

I cleared this exam, and this is my personal opinion.

Regards,

Karthik Arjun

entry.title: In BOM SKU 8012092 SAP Spatial Asset Management for S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Team,
WE have a BOM in which the following 8012092 SAP Spatial Asset Management for S/4HANA for priv tailored option.

So do you know  is this  solution needs the connector special or optimaizer server (infra) ?

Thanks  for your time

DG
